NKJV.1: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits, whether they are of God; because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
NKJV.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God,
NKJV.3: and every spirit that does not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is not of God. And this is the spirit of the Antichrist, which you have heard was coming, and is now already in the world.
NKJV.4: You are of God, little children, and have overcome them, because He who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.
NKJV.5: They are of the world. Therefore they speak as of the world, and the world hears them.
NKJV.6: We are of God. He who knows God hears us; he who is not of God does not hear us. By this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
NKJV.7: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is of God; and everyone who loves is born of God and knows God.
NKJV.8: He who does not love does not know God, for God is love.
NKJV.9: In this the love of God was manifested toward us, that God has sent His only begotten Son into the world, that we might live through Him.
NKJV.10: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that He loved us and sent His Son to be the propitiation for our sins.
NKJV.11: Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
NKJV.12: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God abides in us, and His love has been perfected in us.
NKJV.13: By this we know that we abide in Him, and He in us, because He has given us of His Spirit.
NKJV.14: And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son as Savior of the world.
NKJV.15: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him, and he in God.
NKJV.16: And we have known and believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and he who abides in love abides in God, and God in him.
NKJV.17: Love has been perfected among us in this: that we may have boldness in the day of judgment; because as He is, so are we in this world.
NKJV.18: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear involves torment. But he who fears has not been made perfect in love.
NKJV.19: We love Him because He first loved us.
NKJV.20: If someone says, “I love God,” and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who does not love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen?
NKJV.21: And this commandment we have from Him: that he who loves God must love his brother also.

ESV.1: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world.
ESV.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,
ESV.3: and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you heard was coming and now is in the world already.
ESV.4: Little children, you are from God and have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.
ESV.5: They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world, and the world listens to them.
ESV.6: We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us; whoever is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
ESV.7: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God, and whoever loves has been born of God and knows God.
ESV.8: Anyone who does not love does not know God, because God is love.
ESV.9: In this the love of God was made manifest among us, that God sent his only Son into the world, so that we might live through him.
ESV.10: In this is love, not that we have loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins.
ESV.11: Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
ESV.12: No one has ever seen God; if we love one another, God abides in us and his love is perfected in us.
ESV.13: By this we know that we abide in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
ESV.14: And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent his Son to be the Savior of the world.
ESV.15: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him, and he in God.
ESV.16: So we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.
ESV.17: By this is love perfected with us, so that we may have confidence for the day of judgment, because as he is so also are we in this world.
ESV.18: There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear. For fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not been perfected in love.
ESV.19: We love because he first loved us.
ESV.20: If anyone says, “I love God,” and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who does not love his brother whom he has seen cannot  love God whom he has not seen.
ESV.21: And this commandment we have from him: whoever loves God must also love his brother.

NASB.1: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
NASB.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God;
NASB.3: and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God; this is the [spirit] of the antichrist, of which you have heard that it is coming, and now it is already in the world.
NASB.4: You are from God, little children, and have overcome them; because greater is He who is in you than he who is in the world.
NASB.5: They are from the world; therefore they speak [as] from the world, and the world listens to them.
NASB.6: We are from God; he who knows God listens to us; he who is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
NASB.7: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God; and everyone who loves is born of God and knows God.
NASB.8: The one who does not love does not know God, for God is love.
NASB.9: By this the love of God was manifested in us, that God has sent His only begotten Son into the world so that we might live through Him.
NASB.10: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that He loved us and sent His Son [to be] the propitiation for our sins.
NASB.11: Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
NASB.12: No one has seen God at any time; if we love one another, God abides in us, and His love is perfected in us.
NASB.13: By this we know that we abide in Him and He in us, because He has given us of His Spirit.
NASB.14: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son [to be] the Savior of the world.
NASB.15: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him, and he in God.
NASB.16: We have come to know and have believed the love which God has for us. God is love, and the one who abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.
NASB.17: By this, love is perfected with us, so that we may have confidence in the day of judgment; because as He is, so also are we in this world.
NASB.18: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear involves punishment, and the one who fears is not perfected in love.
NASB.19: We love, because He first loved us.
NASB.20: If someone says, 'I love God,' and hates his brother, he is a liar; for the one who does not love his brother whom he has seen, cannot love God whom he has not seen.
NASB.21: And this commandment we have from Him, that the one who loves God should love his brother also.

NLT.1: Dear friends, do not believe everyone who claims to speak by the Spirit. You must test them to see if the spirit they have comes from God. For there are many false prophets in the world.
NLT.2: This is how we know if they have the Spirit of God: If a person claiming to be a prophet acknowledges that Jesus Christ came in a real body, that person has the Spirit of God.
NLT.3: But if someone claims to be a prophet and does not acknowledge the truth about Jesus, that person is not from God. Such a person has the spirit of the Antichrist, which you heard is coming into the world and indeed is already here.
NLT.4: But you belong to God, my dear children. You have already won a victory over those people, because the Spirit who lives in you is greater than the spirit who lives in the world.
NLT.5: Those people belong to this world, so they speak from the world’s viewpoint, and the world listens to them.
NLT.6: But we belong to God, and those who know God listen to us. If they do not belong to God, they do not listen to us. That is how we know if someone has the Spirit of truth or the spirit of deception.
NLT.7: Dear friends, let us continue to love one another, for love comes from God. Anyone who loves is a child of God and knows God.
NLT.8: But anyone who does not love does not know God, for God is love.
NLT.9: God showed how much he loved us by sending his one and only Son into the world so that we might have eternal life through him.
NLT.10: This is real love — not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as a sacrifice to take away our sins.
NLT.11: Dear friends, since God loved us that much, we surely ought to love each other.
NLT.12: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is brought to full expression in us.
NLT.13: And God has given us his Spirit as proof that we live in him and he in us.
NLT.14: Furthermore, we have seen with our own eyes and now testify that the Father sent his Son to be the Savior of the world.
NLT.15: All who declare that Jesus is the Son of God have God living in them, and they live in God.
NLT.16: We know how much God loves us, and we have put our trust in his love. God is love, and all who live in love live in God, and God lives in them.
NLT.17: And as we live in God, our love grows more perfect. So we will not be afraid on the day of judgment, but we can face him with confidence because we live like Jesus here in this world.
NLT.18: Such love has no fear, because perfect love expels all fear. If we are afraid, it is for fear of punishment, and this shows that we have not fully experienced his perfect love.
NLT.19: We love each other because he loved us first.
NLT.20: If someone says, “I love God,” but hates a fellow believer, that person is a liar; for if we don’t love people we can see, how can we love God, whom we cannot see?
NLT.21: And he has given us this command: Those who love God must also love their fellow believers.

WEB.1: Beloved, don’t believe every spirit, but test the spirits, whether they are of God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
WEB.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit who confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God,
WEB.3: and every spirit who doesn’t confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is not of God, and this is the spirit of the Antichrist, of whom you have heard that it comes. Now it is in the world already.
WEB.4: You are of God, little children, and have overcome them; because greater is he who is in you than he who is in the world.
WEB.5: They are of the world. Therefore they speak of the world, and the world hears them.
WEB.6: We are of God. He who knows God listens to us. He who is not of God doesn’t listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.
WEB.7: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is of God; and everyone who loves has been born of God, and knows God.
WEB.8: He who doesn’t love doesn’t know God, for God is love.
WEB.9: By this God’s love was revealed in us, that God has sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.
WEB.10: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son as the atoning sacrifice  for our sins.
WEB.11: Beloved, if God loved us in this way, we also ought to love one another.
WEB.12: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God remains in us, and his love has been perfected in us.
WEB.13: By this we know that we remain in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
WEB.14: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son as the Savior of the world.
WEB.15: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God remains in him, and he in God.
WEB.16: We know and have believed the love which God has for us. God is love, and he who remains in love remains in God, and God remains in him.
WEB.17: In this love has been made perfect among us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because as he is, even so are we in this world.
WEB.18: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment. He who fears is not made perfect in love.
WEB.19: We love him, because he first loved us.
WEB.20: If a man says, “I love God,” and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who doesn’t love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen?
WEB.21: This commandment we have from him, that he who loves God should also love his brother.

CSB17.1: Dear friends, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits <sup>ⓐ</sup> to see if they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
CSB17.2: This is how you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh <sup>ⓑ</sup> is from God, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.3: but every spirit that does not confess Jesus <sup>[1]</sup> is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, <sup>ⓓ</sup> which you have heard is coming; even now it is already in the world.
CSB17.4: You are from God, little children, and you have conquered them, because the one who is in you <sup>ⓔ</sup> is greater than the one who is in the world. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.5: They are from the world. <sup>ⓖ</sup> Therefore what they say is from the world, and the world listens to them.
CSB17.6: We are from God. Anyone who knows God listens to us; <sup>ⓗ</sup> anyone who is not from God does not listen to us. This is how we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of deception. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.7: Dear friends, let us love one another, because love is from God, and everyone who loves has been born of God <sup>ⓙ</sup> and knows God.
CSB17.8: The one who does not love does not know God, because God is love.
CSB17.9: God’s love was revealed among us <sup>[2]</sup> in this way: God sent <sup>ⓚ</sup> his one and only Son <sup>ⓛ</sup> into the world so that we might live <sup>ⓜ</sup> through him.
CSB17.10: Love consists in this: not that we loved God, but that he loved us <sup>ⓝ</sup> and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup> for our sins.
CSB17.11: Dear friends, if God loved us in this way, we also must love one another. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.12: No one has ever seen God. <sup>ⓒ</sup> If we love one another, God remains in <sup>[4]</sup> us and his love is made complete in us.
CSB17.13: This is how we know that we remain in him <sup>ⓓ</sup> and he in us: He has given us of his Spirit. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.14: And we have seen and we testify that the Father has sent his Son <sup>ⓕ</sup> as the world’s Savior.
CSB17.15: Whoever confesses <sup>ⓖ</sup> that Jesus is the Son of God — God remains in him and he in God.
CSB17.16: And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and the one who remains in love remains in God, and God remains in him.
CSB17.17: In this, love is made complete with us so that we may have confidence in the day of judgment, <sup>ⓗ</sup> because as he is, so also are we in this world. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.18: There is no fear in love; instead, perfect love drives out fear, <sup>ⓙ</sup> because fear involves punishment. <sup>[5]</sup> So the one who fears is not complete in love. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: We love <sup>[6]</sup> because he first loved us. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.20: If anyone says, “I love God,” and yet hates his brother or sister, <sup>ⓜ</sup> he is a liar. <sup>ⓝ</sup> For the person who does not love his brother or sister <sup>ⓐ</sup> whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen. <sup>[7]</sup>
CSB17.21: And we have this command from him: The one who loves God must also love his brother and sister.

AMP.1: Beloved, do not believe every spirit [speaking through a self-proclaimed prophet]; instead test the spirits to see whether they are from God, because many false prophets and teachers have gone out into the world.
AMP.2: By this you know and recognize the Spirit of God: every spirit that acknowledges and confesses [the fact] that Jesus Christ has [actually] come in the flesh [as a man] is from God [God is its source];
AMP.3: and every spirit that does not confess Jesus [acknowledging that He has come in the flesh, but would deny any of the Son's true nature] is not of God; this is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming, and is now already in the world.
AMP.4: Little children (believers, dear ones), you are of God and you belong to Him and have [already] overcome them [the agents of the antichrist]; because He who is in you is greater than he (Satan) who is in the world [of sinful mankind].
AMP.5: They [who teach twisted doctrine] are of the world and belong to it; therefore they speak from the [viewpoint of the] world [with its immoral freedom and baseless theories—demanding compliance with their opinions and ridiculing the values of the upright], and the [gullible one of the] world listens closely and pays attention to them.
AMP.6: We [who teach God's word] are from God [energized by the Holy Spirit], and whoever knows God [through personal experience] listens to us [and has a deeper understanding of Him]. Whoever is not of God does not listen to us. By this we know [without any doubt] the spirit of truth [motivated by God] and the spirit of error [motivated by Satan].
AMP.7: Beloved, let us [unselfishly] love and seek the best for one another, for love is from God; and everyone who loves [others] is born of God and knows God [through personal experience].
AMP.8: The one who does not love has not become acquainted with God [does not and never did know Him], for God is love. [He is the originator of love, and it is an enduring attribute of His nature.]
AMP.9: By this the love of God was displayed in us, in that God has sent His [One and] only begotten Son [the One who is truly unique, the only One of His kind] into the world so that we might live through Him.
AMP.10: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that He loved us and sent His Son to be the propitiation [that is, the atoning sacrifice, and the satisfying offering] for our sins [fulfilling God's requirement for justice against sin and placating His wrath].
AMP.11: Beloved, if God so loved us [in this incredible way], we also ought to love one another.
AMP.12: No one has seen God at any time. But if we love one another [with unselfish concern], God abides in us, and His love [the love that is His essence abides in us and] is completed and perfected in us.
AMP.13: By this we know [with confident assurance] that we abide in Him and He in us, because He has given to us His [Holy] Spirit.
AMP.14: We [who were with Him in person] have seen and testify [as eye-witnesses] that the Father has sent the Son to be the Savior of the world.
AMP.15: Whoever confesses and acknowledges that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him, and he in God.
AMP.16: We have come to know [by personal observation and experience], and have believed [with deep, consistent faith] the love which God has for us. God is love, and the one who abides in love abides in God, and God abides continually in him.
AMP.17: In this [union and fellowship with Him], love is completed and perfected with us, so that we may have confidence in the day of judgment [with assurance and boldness to face Him]; because as He is, so are we in this world.
AMP.18: There is no fear in love [dread does not exist]. But perfect (complete, full-grown) love drives out fear, because fear involves [the expectation of divine] punishment, so the one who is afraid [of God's judgment] is not perfected in love [has not grown into a sufficient understanding of God's love].
AMP.19: We love, because He first loved us.
AMP.20: If anyone says, "I love God," and hates (works against) his [Christian] brother he is a liar; for the one who does not love his brother whom he has seen, cannot love God whom he has not seen.
AMP.21: And this commandment we have from Him, that the one who loves God should also [unselfishly] love his brother and seek the best for him.

MSG.1: My dear friends, don't believe everything you hear. Carefully weigh and examine what people tell you. Not everyone who talks about God comes from God. There are a lot of lying preachers loose in the world.
MSG.2: Here's how you test for the genuine Spirit of God. Everyone who confesses openly his faith in Jesus Christ—the Son of God, who came as an actual flesh-and-blood person—comes from God and belongs to God.
MSG.3: And everyone who refuses to confess faith in Jesus has nothing in common with God. This is the spirit of antichrist that you heard was coming. Well, here it is, sooner than we thought!
MSG.4: My dear children, you come from God and belong to God. You have already won a big victory over those false teachers, for the Spirit in you is far stronger than anything in the world.
MSG.5: These people belong to the Christ-denying world. They talk the world's language and the world eats it up.
MSG.6: But we come from God and belong to God. Anyone who knows God understands us and listens. The person who has nothing to do with God will, of course, not listen to us. This is another test for telling the Spirit of Truth from the spirit of deception.
MSG.7: My beloved friends, let us continue to love each other since love comes from God. Everyone who loves is born of God and experiences a relationship with God.
MSG.8: The person who refuses to love doesn't know the first thing about God, because God is love—so you can't know him if you don't love.
MSG.9: This is how God showed his love for us: God sent his only Son into the world so we might live through him.
MSG.10: This is the kind of love we are talking about—not that we once upon a time loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as a sacrifice to clear away our sins and the damage they've done to our relationship with God.
MSG.11: My dear, dear friends, if God loved us like this, we certainly ought to love each other.
MSG.12: No one has seen God, ever. But if we love one another, God dwells deeply within us, and his love becomes complete in us—perfect love!
MSG.13: This is how we know we're living steadily and deeply in him, and he in us: He's given us life from his life, from his very own Spirit.
MSG.14: Also, we've seen for ourselves and continue to state openly that the Father sent his Son as Savior of the world.
MSG.15: Everyone who confesses that Jesus is God's Son participates continuously in an intimate relationship with God.
MSG.16: We know it so well, we've embraced it heart and soul, this love that comes from God. God is love. When we take up permanent residence in a life of love, we live in God and God lives in us.
MSG.17: This way, love has the run of the house, becomes at home and mature in us, so that we're free of worry on Judgment Day—our standing in the world is identical with Christ's.
MSG.18: There is no room in love for fear. Well-formed love banishes fear. Since fear is crippling, a fearful life—fear of death, fear of judgment—is one not yet fully formed in love.
MSG.19: We, though, are going to love—love and be loved. First we were loved, now we love. He loved us first.
MSG.20: If anyone boasts, "I love God," and goes right on hating his brother or sister, thinking nothing of it, he is a liar. If he won't love the person he can see, how can he love the God he can't see?
MSG.21: The command we have from Christ is blunt: Loving God includes loving people. You've got to love both.

NIV.1:
Test the Spirits
Dear friends, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
NIV.2: This is how you can recognize the Spirit of God: Every spirit that acknowledges that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,
NIV.3: but every spirit that does not acknowledge Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and even now is already in the world.
NIV.4: You, dear children, are from God and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
NIV.5: They are from the world and therefore speak from the viewpoint of the world, and the world listens to them.
NIV.6: We are from God, and whoever knows God listens to us; but whoever is not from God does not listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of falsehood.
NIV.7:
God’s Love and Ours
Dear friends, let us love one another, for love comes from God. Everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God.
NIV.8: Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love.
NIV.9: This is how God showed his love among us: He sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.
NIV.10: This is love: not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins.
NIV.11: Dear friends, since God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
NIV.12: No one has ever seen God; but if we love one another, God lives in us and his love is made complete in us.
NIV.13: We know that we live in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
NIV.14: And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent his Son to be the Savior of the world.
NIV.15: If anyone acknowledges that Jesus is the Son of God, God lives in him and he in God.
NIV.16:
And so we know and rely on the love God has for us.
God is love. Whoever lives in love lives in God, and God in him.
NIV.17: In this way, love is made complete among us so that we will have confidence on the day of judgment, because in this world we are like him.
NIV.18: There is no fear in love. But perfect love drives out fear, because fear has to do with punishment. The one who fears is not made perfect in love.
NIV.19: We love because he first loved us.
NIV.20: If anyone says, “I love God,” yet hates his brother, he is a liar. For anyone who does not love his brother, whom he has seen, cannot love God, whom he has not seen.
NIV.21: And he has given us this command: Whoever loves God must also love his brother.

NRSVCE.1: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God; for many false prophets have gone out into the world.
NRSVCE.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,
NRSVCE.3: and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. And this is the spirit of the antichrist, of which you have heard that it is coming; and now it is already in the world.
NRSVCE.4: Little children, you are from God, and have conquered them; for the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
NRSVCE.5: They are from the world; therefore what they say is from the world, and the world listens to them.
NRSVCE.6: We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us, and whoever is not from God does not listen to us. From this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
NRSVCE.7: Beloved, let us love one another, because love is from God; everyone who loves is born of God and knows God.
NRSVCE.8: Whoever does not love does not know God, for God is love.
NRSVCE.9: God’s love was revealed among us in this way: God sent his only Son into the world so that we might live through him.
NRSVCE.10: In this is love, not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.
NRSVCE.11: Beloved, since God loved us so much, we also ought to love one another.
NRSVCE.12: No one has ever seen God; if we love one another, God lives in us, and his love is perfected in us.
NRSVCE.13: By this we know that we abide in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
NRSVCE.14: And we have seen and do testify that the Father has sent his Son as the Savior of the world.
NRSVCE.15: God abides in those who confess that Jesus is the Son of God, and they abide in God.
NRSVCE.16: So we have known and believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who abide in love abide in God, and God abides in them.
NRSVCE.17: Love has been perfected among us in this: that we may have boldness on the day of judgment, because as he is, so are we in this world.
NRSVCE.18: There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear; for fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not reached perfection in love.
NRSVCE.19: We love because he first loved us.
NRSVCE.20: Those who say, “I love God,” and hate their brothers or sisters, are liars; for those who do not love a brother or sister whom they have seen, cannot love God whom they have not seen.
NRSVCE.21: The commandment we have from him is this: those who love God must love their brothers and sisters also.

NET.1: Dear friends, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to determine if they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.
NET.2: By this you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit that confesses Jesus as the Christ who has come in the flesh is from God,
NET.3: but every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God, and this is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming, and now is already in the world.
NET.4: You are from God, little children, and have conquered them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
NET.5: They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world’s perspective and the world listens to them.
NET.6: We are from God; the person who knows God listens to us, but whoever is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of deceit.
NET.7: Dear friends, let us love one another, because love is from God, and everyone who loves has been fathered by God and knows God.
NET.8: The person who does not love does not know God, because God is love.
NET.9: By this the love of God is revealed in us: that God has sent his one and only Son into the world so that we may live through him.
NET.10: In this is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.
NET.11: Dear friends, if God so loved us, then we also ought to love one another.
NET.12: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God resides in us, and his love is perfected in us.
NET.13: By this we know that we reside in God and he in us: in that he has given us of his Spirit.
NET.14: And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the Savior of the world.
NET.15: If anyone confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God resides in him and he in God.
NET.16: And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has in us. God is love, and the one who resides in love resides in God, and God resides in him.
NET.17: By this love is perfected with us, so that we may have confidence in the day of judgment, because just as Jesus is, so also are we in this world.
NET.18: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear has to do with punishment. The one who fears punishment has not been perfected in love.
NET.19: We love because he loved us first.
NET.20: If anyone says “I love God” and yet hates his fellow Christian, he is a liar, because the one who does not love his fellow Christian whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen.
NET.21: And the commandment we have from him is this: that the one who loves God should love his fellow Christian too.
